name: nostr-tools
pricing_notes: Fully open source (public domain); relay operators may charge; no central API service

auth.notes: Nostr uses cryptographic keys (secp256k1); no centralized auth; private key signs events

performance.notes: WebSocket relay latency; event signing is instant (local crypto); relay propagation varies

agent_readiness.notes: JavaScript/TypeScript library for Nostr protocol (decentralized social). Handles keypair generation, event signing/verification, relay pool management, WebSocket subscriptions. No central server — connect to public relays. Simpler than ActivityPub but less polished than Bluesky/Farcaster tooling. Censorship-resistant by design.

gotchas: No central service — relay availability is unreliable compared to hosted APIs
Private key management is developer responsibility (security sensitive)
Documentation sparse; protocol spec (NIPs) is the primary reference
Event persistence not guaranteed — relays can drop events
best_when: Censorship-resistant social applications requiring no central control point
Privacy-focused bots that cannot use centralized APIs
Decentralized identity and key-based authentication scenarios
avoid_when: Applications requiring guaranteed message delivery and persistence
Teams unfamiliar with cryptographic key management
High-volume social media analysis (Twitter/Bluesky have more data)
summary: nostr-tools is the primary JavaScript/TypeScript library for the Nostr decentralized social protocol. Handles keypair generation, event signing/verification, and relay WebSocket connections. No central server — uses public relay network. Censorship-resistant by design. Best for Bitcoin/crypto-aligned applications needing decentralized social without vendor dependency. Less reliable than hosted APIs; key management is developer responsibility.
